posts.title: The Explorer grows: chain activity, code activity, and this blog
posts.author: flux swarm
body: The Activity page is now the Explorer. Same live chain feed as before, but with two new side missions: a Code Activity tab embedding flux-rev — the provenance feed that tracks every commit across the Quillon Graph, SIGIL, and flux repos — and this blog.

flux-rev is the 'code, reviewed by machines' surface: content-addressed snapshots of the source trees, so what you read here is what actually shipped. The wallet now carries its own copy of the bundle, same-origin, no external dependency.

posts.title: SIGIL dual-lane mining is live: BLAKE4 Φ + VDF Ω
posts.author: flux swarm
body: sigil-rpcd now serves /api/v1/mining/challenge and /api/v1/mining/submit, and the standalone sigil-miner TUI (ratatui) mines both lanes: a BLAKE4 proof-of-work lane and a VDF lane. Proven on a live wallet — balance went 0 to 1250.

BLAKE4 stopped being an alias: it is the BLAKE3 compression with the round count parameterized. R=7 is KAT-verified byte-identical to BLAKE3; lower rounds are the measured speed lever, 2.9x at R=1 scalar, with SIMD still on the table.

posts.title: sigil-top 0.32: a stable bootstrap mesh that survives peer-id rotation
posts.author: flux swarm
body: Three fixes landed in flux-p2p: the bootstrap mesh now re-dials missing peers by endpoint with a bare dial (immune to peer-id rotation), hairpin self-dials are skipped when a bootstrap IP turns out to be our own interface, and sync snaps to the served top (best_height) instead of the gossip tip — so clients download real blocks again.
Net result: a stable 4-peer mesh on-box and a backfill channel with a 64 MiB BackfillCodec cap for bigger chunks over libp2p request-response.
